data.title: Sanofi, Regeneron's Itepekimab Delivers Mixed Results in Late-Stage Trials
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Sanofi and Regeneron said that drug candidate Itepekimab met the primary goal in one of two chronic obstructive pulmonary disease phase 3 studies, but didn't hit the main objective of a second trial.

The French pharmaceutical company on Friday said that Itepekimab in former smokers with inadequately controlled chronic obstructive pulmonary disease met the primary endpoint of a statistically significant reduction in moderate or severe acute exacerbations compared to placebo of 27% at week 52.

However, a second phase three study didn't meet primary endpoint, although a benefit was seen earlier in the trial, it said.

Itepekimab was generally well tolerated in both studies. Sanofi and Regeneron are assessing the data and will discuss with regulatory authorities to evaluate next steps, it said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Jiangsu Lopal Tech to Inject Additional Capital into Lithium Iron Phosphate Cathode Materials Subsidiary
data.description:
Jiangsu Lopal Tech will boost its capital contribution in non-wholly owned subsidiary Changzhou Liyuan New Energy Technology by 369.7 million yuan, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The capital injection will allow the lubricant manufacturer to subscribe for about 55.5 million yuan in Changzhou Liyuan's registered capital, the filing said.

Changzhou Liyuan shareholders waived their pre-emptive rights to the capital increase, the filing said.

Upon completion of the capital injection, Jiangsu Lopal Tech's shareholding in Changzhou Liyuan will rise to 66.42% from 64.03%, the filing said.

Changzhou Liyuan produces and sells LFP or lithium iron phosphate cathode materials.

data.title: Finnish GDP Stalls in Q1
data.description:
Finland’s economy stalled quarter-on-quarter in the first three months of 2025, compared to initial estimates of a 0.1% gain and following an upwardly revised 0.2% expansion in the previous quarter.

Household consumption rebounded, rising by 0.5% after a 0.5% decline in Q4 2024, while fixed investment also recovered, increasing by 0.3% following a 0.2% drop.

In contrast, government spending contracted by 2% in Q1 2025, reversing a 0.6% growth in the prior quarter.

In terms of external trade, exports of goods and services fell at a slower pace (-0.4% vs -1.9% in Q4), while imports (0.2% vs -1.4%) rebounded.

On an annual basis, Finland’s GDP grew by 0.8% in Q1 2025, down from an initial estimate of 1.2% advance and slowing from a downwardly revised 1% growth recorded in Q4 2024.

data.title: Base metals fall as dollar firms, US tariff optimism fades
data.description:
Industrial metals fell on Friday as the dollar strengthened and market optimism faded following a court ruling that reinstated the broadest tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump.

Three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was down 0.1% at $9,562.50 per metric ton, as of 0521 GMT.

The red metal, used in power and construction, has still gained 4.8% so far this month, and is set for its best month since September 2024.

The most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) shed 0.3% to 77,740 yuan ($10,813) per ton.

LME aluminium fell 0.2% to $2,445 a ton, zinc slipped 0.7% to $2,656.50, lead dipped 0.6% to $1,952 and nickel fell 0.2% to $15,335. Tin dropped 1.4% to $30,790.

"Market rose yesterday due to optimism that the court was able to block Trump tariffs, but the rally faded later as the appeals court suspended that verdict," a metals trader in Singapore said.

A federal appeals court temporarily reinstated the most sweeping of Trump's tariffs on Thursday, a day after a U.S. trade court ruled that the president had exceeded his authority in imposing the duties and ordered an immediate block on them.

Stocks slipped in Asia as investors digested the court's move to keep Trump's tariffs in effect.

Meanwhile, the dollar index rose 0.2% against its rivals, making dollar-denominated assets more expensive to holders of other currencies.

Investors await the April U.S. personal consumption expenditures (PCE) price index report — the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge — due later in the day, which could provide further insight into the central bank's policy direction.

SHFE aluminium eased 0.2% to 20,110 yuan a ton, lead dropped 0.8% to 16,630 yuan, nickel rose 0.9% to 120,810 yuan, while zinc lost 0.5% to 22,250 yuan and tin fell 2.6% to 251,120 yuan.

($1 = 7.1890 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: CBOT soybeans may fall into $10.31-3/4 to $10.37 range
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean July contract (SN25) may break support of $10.41-3/4 per bushel, and fall into $10.31-3/4 to $10.37 range.

A wave C from $10.73-1/4 has briefly travelled below its 86.4% projection level of $10.41-3/4. Chances are it may extend to the 100% level of $10.37.

Most of the bounce triggered by the support has been reversed. The reversal suggests a continuation of the wave C. Immediate resistance is at $10.45-1/2, a break above which could lead to a gain into $10.50-3/4 to $10.55 range.

On the daily chart, the pattern from the December 2024 low of $9.66 looks like a wedge. Three waves make up the pattern. The wave C and the wave A are closely related, as revealed by a projection analysis.

The lower trendline of the wedge points at a target of $10.05-1/2.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.​

data.title: Bangkok Dusit Medical Services' Earnings Outlook Appears Softer — Market Talk
data.description: Bangkok Dusit Medical Services' earnings outlook appears softer, Maybank Securities (Thailand)'s Nontapat Sahakitpinyo says in a research report. Management has guided for 2025 revenue growth of 4%-6%, down from its earlier 7%-8% growth target for 1H 2025, which probably reflects a weaker economic outlook for Thailand, the analyst says. Management also noted Middle East revenue may decline in the long term as countries in the region invest in their own healthcare systems, the analyst says. The brokerage reduces its 2025-2027 earnings estimates for the Thai hospital operator by 2%-5%. It lowers the stock's target price to THB27.50 from THB31.00 with an unchanged buy rating. Shares are unchanged at THB21.60. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Indian Rupee opens higher against the US dollar
data.description: In an early morning trade on Friday, the Indian rupee opened 17 paise higher against the US dollar at 85.35, amid stronger domestic equity markets.The domestic currency previously closed at 85.52 against the U.S. dollar.Meanwhile this morning, the Sensex opened 0.07% higher, rising by 56.85 points, to 81,689.87, while the Nifty gained 0.10 percent, or 25.20 points, to 24,858.80.

data.title: South Africa's Dis-Chem Pharmacies posts annual profit jump, declares final dividend
data.description:
South African pharmacy chain Dis-Chem Pharmacies reported a 20% increase in full-year earnings on Friday, and also declared a final dividend of 27.85 cents.

The group's headline earnings per share, a key profit measure in South Africa, increased to 137.5 cents for the year ended February 28, up from 114.6 cents a year earlier, supported by an 18.3% growth in operating profit.

"The biggest contributor to earnings growth during this period was effective cost management, particularly payroll cost," Dis-Chem said in a statement.

Group revenue increased 8% to 39.2 billion rand ($2.20 billion), with retail revenue rising nearly 6% to 33.6 billion rand and comparable pharmacy store revenue growing 4.2%.

Wholesale revenue grew 9.9%, while wholesale sales to external customers — including The Local Choice franchises and independent clients — jumped 22.1%, driven by new customer acquisitions and stronger support from the existing base, the group said.

($1 = 17.8397 rand)

data.title: Triona is strengthening the board with Halvor Walla
data.description:
Triona welcomes Halvor Walla, with a long and solid background in software and product development, as a member of the board.

Halvor is the founder of Serve ASA and Framsikt AS, the latter now part of Visma. Halvor remains active as Product Director at Framsikt and also holds board positions in several companies within the Visma Group. Previously, he has served as a board member of Gatsoft AS and held senior positions at Agresso Group ASA (now Unit4), ErgoGroup ASA (part of TietoEvry), and Software Innovation ASA (also now part of TietoEvry).

“We are very pleased to welcome Halvor to Triona’s board. His experience and insights will be valuable in our continued growth journey,” says Lennart Hane, Chairman of the Board, Triona.

data.title: ProstaLund AB - Interim report January-March 2025
data.description:
Januari 1 - March 31

  • Net sales reached SEK 3,7 million (3,5)
  • Operating result amounted to -5,0 MSEK (-4,7)
  • Profit after tax amounted to -5,0 MSEK (-4,8)
  • Earnings per share before and after dilution amounted to -1,39 SEK (-2,49)
  • Cash flow from operating activities amounted to SEK -4,1 MSEK (-6,6)

Significant events during the period

  • Older men treated with CoreTherm® for benign prostate enlargement have a lower risk of a prostate cancer diagnosis later in life compared to men treated with TURP according to a new Swedish registry study.
  • New study confirms the favourable effect of CoreTherm® in patients with large prostates.
  • Use of Schelin Catheter® before water vapour therapy is cost-effective.

Significant events after the end of the period

  • ProstaLund AB (publ) initiates strategic review due to weakened liquidity and raises loans of SEK 2.5 million.
  • First treatments with CoreTherm® Eagle will be performed next week - im- portant milestone for ProstaLund.
  • Per Langö leaves the board.
  • ProstaLund’s CEO resigns at his own request.
  • First patients treated with CoreTherm® Eagle.
  • ProstaLund AB (publ) comments on trading halt and plans to decide on a directed share issue.
  • ProstaLund AB (publ) has decided to carry out a directed share issue of SEK 5.5 million to several existing shareholders, subject to the subsequent approval of the Annual General Meeting.
  • On May 26, ProstaLund AB appoints Malin Melander as acting CEO.
  • ProstaLund AB (publ) appoints Annette Colin as interim CFO and appoints former CEO Anders Kristensson as consultant in strategic and operational matters.

Key figures

data.title: Change in number of shares and votes in Net Insight
data.description:
In accordance with the resolution passed at the Annual General Meeting on 14 May 2025, Net Insight AB has cancelled 6,838,570 of its own B shares that were previously repurchased by the company.

Following the cancellation, the total number of shares in the company amounts to 341,404,439, of which 1,000,000 are A shares and 340,404,439 are B shares. These shares collectively represent 350,404,439 voting rights in the company. The company’s share capital remains unchanged at SEK 13,929,720.36.

For further information, please contact:Cecilia Höjgård Höök, CFO, +46 8 685 04 00, ir@netinsight.net

About Net Insight

Net Insight (Nasdaq: NETI B) provides the highest performing, most open video transport and media cloud technology for content providers as the industry standard for flexibility and service across live contribution, distribution and remote production media workflows. 

For over 25 years, the world’s leading content owners, broadcasters, production companies, service providers and enterprises have trusted Net Insight’s Emmy® Award winning Nimbra technology to guarantee media delivery. Today, Net Insight partners with hundreds of customers in over 70 countries to ensure media flows across managed and unmanaged IP networks, and the cloud – from anywhere, to everywhere. It enables customers to get the best from any mix of virtualized, cloud and IP technology and is the only platform to support all the major industry standards, protocols and clouds.

For more information, please visit netinsight.net

Follow us:

LinkedIn: linkedin.com/company/net-insight/ Twitter: twitter.com/NetInsight

The information in this press release is such that Net Insight AB (publ) is required to disclose pursuant to the Financial Instruments Trading Act. The information was submitted for publication on 30 May 2025 at hh:mm CEST.

data.title: Conversion of shares in Skanska AB
data.description:
Skanska

According to Skanska AB's articles of association, holders of Class A shares are entitled to submit requests to the company for conversion of their shares to Class B shares. Such conversion reduces the total number of votes in the company.

In conjunction with a conversion of shares, the company is obliged according to the Swedish Financial Instruments Trading Act (1991:980) to disclose information concerning the change.

During May 2025, 1,870 Class A shares were converted to Class B shares at the request of shareholders. Accordingly, the total number of votes in the company thereafter totals 595,810,259.

The total number of registered shares in the company amounts to 419,903,072, of which 19,545,243 are Class A shares and 400,357,829 are Class B shares.

This is information that Skanska AB (publ) is obliged to make public pursuant to the Swedish Financial Instruments Trading Act. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the Senior Vice President Investor Relations set out below, at 07:30 am CEST on May 30, 2025.

For further information please contact:

Antonia Junelind, Senior Vice President Investor Relations, Skanska AB, tel: +46 (0)10 448 62 61

Direct line for media, tel: +46 (0)10 448 88 99

This and previous releases can also be found at www.skanska.com.

Skanska Group uses its knowledge and foresight to shape the way people live, work, and connect. We are one of the world's largest construction and project development companies, with 2024 revenue totaling SEK 177 billion. We operate across select markets in the Nordics, Europe and USA. Together with our customers and the collective expertise of our approximately 26,300 teammates, we create innovative and sustainable solutions that support healthy living beyond our lifetime.

https://news.cision.com/skanska/r/conversion-of-shares-in-skanska-ab%2Cc4151236

data.title: Change in number of shares and votes in Scandic
data.description:
Scandic Hotels Group AB

The number of shares and votes in Scandic Hotels Group AB (publ) (“Scandic”) has changed in May 2025 following the implementation of the resolution passed by the Annual General Meeting on 6 May 2025 to cancel 4,030,622 own shares that had previously been repurchased by Scandic.

The total number of shares in Scandic has in May 2025 decreased by 4,030,622 shares, corresponding to the same number of votes. Today, the last trading day of the month, the total number of shares and votes in Scandic amounts to 215,127,300.

For more information, please contact:

Rasmus Blomqvist, Director Investor Relations, Scandic Hotels Group

Email: rasmus.blomqvist@scandichotels.com

Phone: +46 702 335 367

This information is information that Scandic Hotels Group AB (publ) is obliged to make public pursuant to the Swedish Financial Instruments Trading Act. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact person set out above, at 07:30 CEST on 30 May 2025.

  

About Scandic Hotels Group

Scandic is the largest hotel company in the Nordic countries with a network of about 280 hotels with 58,000 rooms in operation and under development, in more than 130 destinations. The company is the leader when it comes to integrating sustainability in all operations and its award-winning Design for All concept ensures that Scandic hotels are accessible to everyone. Well loved by guests and employees, the Scandic Friends loyalty program is the largest in the Nordic hotel industry and the company is one of the most attractive employers in the region. Scandic is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. www.scandichotelsgroup.com

data.title: OUE, Tokyo Century Break Ground on Hotel Indigo Changi Airport
data.description:
OUE and Tokyo Century broke ground on the Hotel Indigo located at Singapore's Changi Airport on Thursday, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on the same day.

The hotel will have direct access to Terminal 2 and will be linked with the rest of the airport.

The 255-room hotel is expected to be fully operational by 2028, the filing said.

Shares for the two companies were up around 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Commonwealth Bank Restores Account Transfer Services
data.description:
Commonwealth Bank of Australia has resolved an issue that had affected some account transfers via its NetBank and CommBank applications, according to a Friday statement by the Australian bourse.

Customers can now make payments and transfer funds using their PayID or Bank-State-Branch number and account numbers, the statement said.

Card payments and Automatic Teller Machine services were not impacted during the disruption, the statement added.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Spain's Banco Santander Mulling Cryptocurrency Expansion
data.description:
(Updates to add Santander's statement)

Banco Santander is looking into potential offerings to expand in the cryptocurrency space, Bloomberg News reported Thursday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The Spanish bank is considering launching stablecoins denominated in euros and US dollars and whether it should create its own or offer access to existing ones. Plans are, however, in early stages.

Sources also said that Santander's digital banking arm Openbank applied for licenses to provide retail crypto services as soon as this year.

Santander declined to comment when reached by MT Newswires.

data.title: Moriroku Repurchases Shares Worth 663 Million Yen in May
data.description:
Moriroku Holdings repurchased 265,100 shares for 662.8 million yen between May 1 and May 29, through open-market purchases on the Tokyo Stock Exchange, according to a Thursday filing on the Tokyo bourse.

The buyback was part of a program approved on March 13 allowing for up to 600,000 shares or 1 billion yen in repurchases through March 2026.

Moriroku has now repurchased 430,200 shares for a total of (Yen)999.9 million under the program, completing the buyback, program.

data.title: iFAST Secures Direct Access to US Exchanges
data.description:
iFAST secured direct access to US Exchanges, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

This follows the company's subsidiary, iFAST Securities US, receiving an approval to become a participant of The Depository Trust Company (DTC) and a Self-Clearing Member of the National Securities Clearing Corporation (NSCC).

The approval came after the company successfully registered with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (FINRA)

data.title: Texmaco Rail Signs Pact to Collaborate with Rail Vikas Nigam
data.description:
Texmaco Rail & Engineering has entered into a memorandum of understanding with Rail Vikas Nigam to collaborate in areas of mutual interest, especially railway infrastructure and allied sectors, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Under the pact, the companies will jointly work on the manufacture, operation, and maintenance of rolling stock and workshops.

They may also explore setting up a wheel set manufacturing facility and bogie manufacturing plants, manufacturing of metro coaches and trainsets in India, and execution of infrastructure projects, among others.
